framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Publish deep-dive investment research in the open
paths.what_it_is: Post full company valuations or investment theses publicly, with dated, checkable reasoning, instead of keeping analysis inside a firm.
paths.why_it_signals: Aswath Damodaran, Andrew Walker, and John Huber each built outsized reputations by publishing rigorous valuation and investment work for anyone to read and critique.

paths.path: Run a data-driven markets commentary blog
paths.what_it_is: Turn market and portfolio data into a recurring public analysis habit — a blog or podcast — rather than one-off internal memos.
paths.why_it_signals: Ben Carlson, Michael Batnick, and Nick Maggiulli each built a national following and a firm role at Ritholtz Wealth Management from personal blogs that translated data into daily investment commentary.

paths.path: Translate finance and economics for a mass audience
paths.what_it_is: Explain markets and macro developments in plain language for a broad non-specialist audience, not just other analysts.
paths.why_it_signals: Byrne Hobart, Kyla Scanlon, and Matt Levine each turned rigorous financial analysis into some of the most widely-read finance writing in the world, reaching well beyond traditional research readers.
